item.title: 音乐星期一——你在听什么？ | Music Monday — What are you listening to?
item.description:
封面图片来源： artbyjayr <3

我有相当不拘一格的口味，对任何东西都持开放态度，所以我一直很喜欢浏览这些帖子的评论部分并尝试不同的曲调。 我之前什至用分享你最喜欢的万圣节曲调开始了我自己的建议一首歌的帖子。

考虑到这一点，我决定启动一个每周系列，人们可以在其中加入并删除他们正在收听的任何内容的链接 + 浏览其他建议。

那么，你们今天都在听什么？ 注意：您可以使用以下语法在大多数平台上嵌入指向您的歌曲的链接{% embed https://... %} 。

我期待着调到你们的曲调！



原文:

cover image credits: artbyjayr <3

There are loads of posts out there where folks have asked others' for song suggestions and urged the community to drop links to their favorite tunes. I have pretty eclectic tastes and am open to whatever, so I've always enjoyed digging through the comments section of these posts and taste-testing different tunes. I've even kicked off one of my own suggest-a-song posts previously with Share your favorite Halloween tunes.

With that in mind, I've decided to start up a weekly series where folks can chime in and drop links to whatever it is they're listening to + browse others suggestions.

So, what have y'all been listening to today? Note: you can embed a link to your song on most platform using the following syntax {% embed https://... %}.

I look forward to tuning into y'all's tunes!
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 软件开发中远程工作的未来是什么？ | What is the future of remote work in software development?
item.description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于在大流行最严重的时候，世界主要是偏远的。 随着世界上很多地方的情况恢复“正常”，您认为软件行业在远程工作的普及和总体期望方面将如何解决？

Olle Pridiuksson - Olle Pridiuksson - Mar 22 3月22日



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on Apr 25

The world went primarily remote in the height of the pandemic. With things returning to "normal" in a lot of the world, where do you think the software industry will settle in terms of remote work popularity and overall expectations?

Olle Pridiuksson - Mar 22
item.author: ben

item.title: 使用 2 台显示器进行学习和工作 | Use 2 monitors for learning and work
item.description:
在封面图片上，我在第一台显示器上观看 youtube 教程，同时在第二台显示器上编写代码。

自从 3 年前我买了一个额外的显示器插入我的笔记本电脑，我无法想象没有它我自己会做任何严肃的事情。

作为一名 JavaScript 全栈开发人员，我总是学习新事物（前端、后端、移动、桌面、Chrome 扩展、报废、云部署等）

无论是有偿工作还是副业，工作流程大致相同。 为了感觉舒服，我需要同时在全屏 2 个应用程序中保持打开状态。

用例

  1. 我观看视频教程以了解有关新主题的基本想法，并在导师之后重复所有步骤

    • 第一台显示器 – youtube 视频
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

  2. 我阅读文档/书籍并在思维导图中做笔记，以深入理解材料。

    • 第一台显示器 – 文档站点
    • 第二台显示器——思维导图编辑器

  3. 我编码

    • 第一个监视器 - 编码结果（网页或移动应用程序模拟器）
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

当您只有一台显示器时，您应该：

  • 在全屏窗口/应用程序之间切换或
  • 应用程序之间的分屏

在这两种情况下，闪烁的全屏应用程序或管理部分屏幕打开的应用程序的宽度不足会占用您大量的认知能量，并且您会更快地感到疲倦。 此外，您可能会伤害您的眼睛。

所以两个显示器是解决方案。 同时打开的两个全屏应用程序让您在管理信息、吸收新知识和轻松完成工作方面拥有巨大优势。

例如，这就是我使用 2 个监视器对 NextJS 文档进行思维导图的方式：

硬件设置

如果你在谷歌上搜索“多显示器”之类的东西——然后查看图像，你就会对它们的设置产生错误的认识，因为很多图像都是关于水平设置的，这会在一天之内扼杀你的脖子。 相反，您应该选择垂直设置——这样您就可以将眼睛从一台显示器切换到另一台显示器，而不会拉伤脖子。

你不需要一个棘手的小工具架——任何大小合适的罐子或盒子都是合适的。 我多年来一直使用宜家的 Pyssla 罐子作为显示器的支架，它非常好：

操作系统设置

就像谷歌搜索一样，操作系统会将您推向水平设置。 但是，您可以在桌面设置中更改定位。 当操作系统设置跟随现实生活中显示器的位置时很好，这样您就可以直观地工作。

没钱买吗？

即使你是穷人：学生、转行、初入职场、缺钱，我强烈建议你尽快购买便宜的二手显示器，因为它有时会提高你的效率。

也许你已经注意到我的第一张照片：我的显示器很旧，有两条彩色垂直线的缺陷。 我的笔记本电脑很旧，没有钥匙“；/：”。 所以我什至羞于向你展示它们。 但它们一起是学习和工作的酷工具。 我更喜欢这样的一对，而不是我拥有的 mackbook。



原文:

On the cover image I'm watching youtube tutorial on 1st monitor and simultaneously write code in 2nd monitor.

Since I bought an extra monitor to plug into my laptop 3 years ago, I can’t imagine myself doing anything serious without it.

As a JavaScript full-stack developer I always learn new things (Frontend, Backend, Mobile, Desktop, Chrome extensions, Scrapping, Cloud deployment etc.)

Whether it’s a paid job or a side project, the working process is generally the same. And to feel comfortable I need to keep opened in full screen 2 apps at the same time.

Use cases

  1. I watch a video tutorial to get basic ideas about the new subject, and repeat all steps after the tutor

    • 1st monitor – youtube video
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

  2. I read documentation/book and take notes in mindmap for deep understanding of material.

    • 1st monitor – documentation site
    • 2nd monitor – mindmap editor

  3. I code

    • 1st monitor – result of coding (web page or mobile app emulator)
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

When you have only 1 monitor, you should either:

  • switch between full-screen windows/apps or
  • split screen between apps

In both cases, blinking full screen apps or managing lack of width in part screen opened apps will take a lot of your cognitive energy, and you get tired much faster. Also, you can harm your eyes.

So two monitors is the solution. Simultaneously opened, two full-screen apps give you a big advantage to manage information, absorb new knowledge and do your work with fewer efforts.

Example, that's how I mindmaped NextJS docs using 2 monitors:

Hardware setup

If you google something like “multiple monitors” – and look at images, you get the wrong idea about their setup, because lots of images are about Horizontal setup, which will kill your neck in one day. Instead, you should choose a vertical setup – so you can switch your eyes from one monitor to another without straining your neck.

You don’t need for that a tricky gadget holder – any jar or box with proper size is suitable. I use an Ikea's Pyssla jar as a stand for monitor for years, and it is pretty good:

Operational System setup

Just as google search, OS will push you to horizontal setup. However, you can change positioning in desktop settings. That relative position of 1 to 2 affects on how mouse cursor will be moving between monitors. It is good when OS settings follow along with real life monitors’ position, so you will work intuitively.

Have not money for it?

Even if you are a poor: student, career switcher, beginner in job, with lack of money, I highly recommend you to buy even a cheap second hand monitor as soon as possible, because it will increase your efficiency at times.

Maybe you have noticed on the first picture with me: my monitor is old, with defect of two colored vertical lines. And my laptop is old without a key ";/:". So I even ashamed to show them to you. But together they are a cool tool for study and work. I prefer such couple over mackbook that I have.
item.author: apayrus

item.title: Dev.to 越来越危险！ | Dev.to is getting dangerous!
item.description:
当我看到初学者试图通过教学来学习时，我通常会支持（至少我希望如此）。 写一篇文章来帮助自己理解你认为你知道的东西并没有错。

但最近，它变得几乎危险了。 就在今天，我看到了可能非常有害的代码，这些代码可能会弄乱和删除您的文件。 在评论中，我看到一个无知的家伙想使用此代码来组织他的照片。

更糟糕的是什么？ 这篇文章的作者不仅没有警告那个人不要使用他的代码，甚至鼓励他这样做，可能是因为他觉得被承认了。

因此，如果您是这里或其他任何地方的初学者，在尝试其他人的代码时要非常谨慎，尤其是在涉及文件系统时。 永远不要复制和运行代码，即使你认为你理解它。

潜在的危险很难被发现。 在这种特殊情况下，我怀疑代码在重新运行后会删除文件。 不是全部，只有少数。 如果我不尝试以更花哨的方式重新创建程序，我自己不会看到它。

通常，不会导致副作用的编程任务不应危及您的数据。 但问题是初学者可能无法识别潜在的副作用，也无法理解副作用是什么。

我必须重复一遍：保持警惕。 我看到一个讨论，其中一个白痴就如何修复 Linux 生产服务器向新手提供了“有价值的建议”：

不要这样做！ 它可能会删除您的所有数据。 你可以在这里阅读更多

如果您很好奇，新手认为这是认真的建议并做到了！ 所谓的专家为自己辩解，说他不相信有人会这么傻！



原文:

I am usually supportive (at least I hope so) when I see beginners trying to learn by teaching. There is nothing wrong with writing an article to help yourself understand what you think you know.

But lately, it is getting almost dangerous. Just today, I saw potentially very harmful code that can mess and delete your files. In the comments, I saw a clueless guy wanting to use this code to organize his photos.

And what is worse? The author of the article not only did not warn that guy about not using his code but even encouraged him to do so, probably because he felt acknowledged.

So if you are a beginner here or anywhere else, be very cautious when trying someone else's code, especially when a filesystem is involved. Never copy and never run the code, even if you think you understand it.

The potential danger can be hard to spot. In this particular case, I suspect the code deletes files after re-run. Not all of them, just a few. I would not see it myself if I did not try to re-create the program in a more fancy way.

In general, programming tasks that cause no side effects should not endanger your data. But the problem is that beginners would probably not be able to identify potential side effects nor understand what a side effect is.

I have to repeat: stay alert. I saw a discussion in which an idiot offered a "valuable piece of advice" to a newbie on how to fix a Linux production server:

Don't do this! It may delete all your data. You can read more here

If you are curious, the newbie thought it was serious advice and did it! The so-called expert defended himself, saying he didn't believe someone could have been so stupid to do it!
item.author: hanpari

item.title: Como evitar SQL 注入没有 MongoDB | Como evitar SQL Injection no MongoDB
item.description:
回购示例：

https://github.com/williamkoller/my-readings https://github.com/williamkoller/my-readings

Espero ter te ajudado de alguma maneira, pode comentar e compartilhar. Espero ter te ajudado de alguma maneira, pode comentar e compartilhar。



原文:

O que é SQL Injection?

SQL Injection é um ataque no qual código malicioso é passado para um servidor SQL, que o executa. O ataque pode resultar em acesso não autorizado a dados confidenciais, ou destruição de dados críticos.

Como saber se uma query não previne SQL Injection?!

Neste exemplo estou usando CodeQL, o que é CodeQL?!

  • É um mecanismo de análise de código que descobre vulnerabilidades.

Nesse exemplo na aba de security do github:

O code sacanner nos avisa, que se uma consulta de banco de dados (como uma consulta SQL ou NoSQL) for criada a partir de dados fornecidos pelo usuário sem higienização suficiente, um usuário mal-intencionado poderá executar consultas de banco de dados mal-intencionadas.

Como recomendação para queries NoSQL, podemos usar um operador como $eq do MongoDB

Iremos fazer um refactor no UsersRepository para evitar SQL Injection:

Depois de rodar o Github Actions, notamos que não tem mais alerta na aba de security no github:

E assim não temos mais alerta de SQL Injection;

Repo de exemplo:

https://github.com/williamkoller/my-readings

Espero ter te ajudado de alguma maneira, pode comentar e compartilhar.
item.author: williamkoller

item.title: 如何为开源项目做贡献 - 你的第一个 GitHub PR | How to Contribute to Open Source Project - Your First GitHub PR
item.description:
如果您是技术初学者/非技术人员，并且想知道如何为开源项目做出贡献，那么这篇文章适合您。 我将以Bytebase的开源文档为例，指导您完成整个过程。

它是用于数据库模式生命周期管理的 GitLab。 它从 v1.0.2 开始开源其官方文档。

让我们开始吧; 确保您安装了Git并拥有一个GitHub帐户。 如果您对 git 有任何疑问，请咨询 google 或https://git-scm.com/docs 。

准备

  • 访问https://github.com/bytebase/bytebase.com ； 可以看到，其实 Bytebase 开源了整个网站，但是我们只关注 docs/.

  • 在您的项目文件夹下，执行 git clone 以下载整个存储库。

  • 是的，现在我们有了代码，这里是最流行的编辑器 code.visualstudio.com

  • 从 VSCode 打开文件夹

删除文件

执行删除

  • 我们的第一个示例是删除一个页面 - Bytebase 取消了v1.0.0中的一项功能，但相关文件仍然存在。

  • 在编辑器中，它非常简单——删除这个文件。

提交和推送

  • 删除后，我们用终端查看当前状态——文件被删除了，对，没错！

  • 更改已提交，但这还不够； 我们需要将其同步到远程分支，以便其他人可以看到。 而且......我们遇到了这个！

  • 这是因为我们现在在主分支。 如果我们构建自己的代码仓库，直接推送到主分支就可以了。 但是现在我们正在尝试对其他人的项目进行更改。 让我们在此处获取文档指南。 创建公关
    • 首先，我们需要创建一个新分支来将我们的本地副本推送到远程。 如果你和我一样——对 git 命令不太熟悉，google 随时为你服务。

    • 我们使用命令“git checkout -b xxx”来创建我们的分支

    • 设置上游并推送到远程“git push --set-upstream origin remove-sql-rollback-page”。

    • 让我们回到 GitHub； 单击“拉取请求”选项卡

    • 我们可以看到推送，但是让我们点击“New pull request”

    • 在这里我们可以看到我们的分支，点击！

    在这里，您可以进行比较，但对于我们的案例来说，这是纯粹的删除。 点击“创建拉取请求”！

    更新文件

    进行更新

    • 我们的第二种情况是更新。 当我们从谷歌文档中复制图片时，它会有自己生成的链接。 我们需要做的是用同一存储库中的 static/doc-assets/xxxx.png 替换它。

    • 首先，我下载所有相关图像并将它们移动到 static/docs-assets/

    • 文件名应加前缀以保持在一起

    • 然后更新相应的文件。 我将旧文件保留在那里以确保新图像看起来相同。

    • 执行 git status 查看状态。

    本地预览

    • 我们运行“pnpm i & pnpm dev”，它编译成功。 现在我们可以访问http://localhost:3000并预览整个站点。

    新公关

    • 对我来说一切都很好，现在让我们创建另一个分支，然后创建一个新 PR

    变更请求

    • 然后，我收到了我的第一个“更改请求”。

    • 是的！ 我忘了推送所有的图像......还有另一个提交和推送。

    • 删除字母“dddd”。

    • 然后，我需要再次向他们请求评论。

    LGTM and Merge LGTM 和合并



原文:

If you are a tech beginner / non-tech person and wonder how to contribute to an open-source project, this is THE article for you. I'll take Bytebase's open-sourced documentation as an example to guide you through the whole process.

Bytebase is an open-source database tool for DBAs and developers; it's the GitLab for database schema lifecycle management. It open-sourced its official documentation since v1.0.2.

Let's get started; make sure you install Git and have a GitHub account. If you have any questions about git, ask google or https://git-scm.com/docs.

Preparation

  • Visit https://github.com/bytebase/bytebase.com; as we can see, actually Bytebase open-sourced the whole website, but we only pay attention to docs/.

  • Under your project folder, do git clone to download the whole repository.

  • Yes, now we have the code, and here is the most popular editor code.visualstudio.com

  • Open the folder from VSCode

Delete a file

Do the Delete

  • Our first example is to remove a page - Bytebase sunset a feature in v1.0.0, but the related file is still there.

  • Within the editor, it's super straightforward - delete this file.

Commit and Push

  • After deletion, we use Terminal to view the current status - the file is deleted, yes, that's right!

  • Git commit with the message telling what this commit is for.

  • The changes are committed, and that's not enough; we need to synchronize it to the remote branch so that others can see. And ... We encountered this!

  • It's because we are now in the main branch. If we build our own code repository, it's OK to push directly to the main branch. But now we are trying to make changes to others' project. Let's get to the document guidance here. https://bytebase.com/docs/document-write-guide

Create a PR

  • "Create a new PR" - how? First, we need to create a new branch to push our local copy to remote. If you are similar to me - not so familiar with the git command, google is always there for you.

  • We use the command "git checkout -b xxx" to create our branch

  • Set the upstream and push to remote "git push --set-upstream origin remove-sql-rollback-page".

  • Let's get back to GitHub; click the "Pull requests" tab

  • We can see the push, but let's click "New pull request"

  • Here we can see our branch, click!

Here, you can make the comparison, but it's pure deletion for our case. Click "Create pull request"!

Update a file

Do the Update

  • Our second case is to update. When we copy images from google docs, it will have its self-generated link. What we need to do is to replace that with static/doc-assets/xxxx.png from the same repository.

  • Firstly, I download all the related images and move these to static/docs-assets/

  • The file names should be prefixed to stay together

  • Then update the corresponding file. I keep the old files there to ensure the new images look the same.

  • Do git status to view the status.

Preview Locally

  • We run "pnpm i & pnpm dev" and it compiles successfully. Now we can visit http://localhost:3000 and preview the whole site.

New PR

  • Everything seems fine to me, now let's create another branch and then a new PR

Change Requests

  • Then, I got my first "change request".

  • Yes! I forgot to push all the images ... And another commit and push.

  • Remove the letters "dddd".

  • Then, I need to re-request reviews from them again.

LGTM and Merge
item.author: adela_bytebase

item.title: JavaScript 面试题 | JavaScript Interview questions
item.description:
该博客是关于我在面试期间或从其他候选人那里遇到的流行且独特的面试问题。
这可能主要包括与 Vanilla Javascript 相关的 Web 开发技术堆栈，还包括 React js、Node js、TypeScript、Angular、HTML 和 CSS

我们走吧！！！ -> Java Script 1) Event bubbling and event capturing and event delegation 2) Function Borrowing => call , apply, bind => create your own call, apply, bind prototype 3) Create your own map in js 4) strict mode -> Java Script 1) 事件冒泡和事件捕获和事件委托 2) 函数借用 => 调用、应用、绑定 => 创建自己的调用、应用、绑定原型 3) 在 js 中创建自己的地图 4) 严格模式

5) inspect tab 5）检查选项卡

-> React js 1) control component and uncontrol components 2) create your own custom hook -> React js 1) 控制组件和非控制组件 2) 创建自己的自定义钩子

3) diff usememo , usecallback and memo 3) 区分 usememo 、 usecallback 和 memo



原文:

The blog is regarding popular and unique interview question which I come across during interview or from other candidates.
This might include tech stack of web dev related to Vanilla Javascript majourly and also include React js, Node js, TypeScript, Angular, HTML and CSS

Lets go!!! -> Java Script 1) Event bubbling and event capturing and event delegation 2) Function Borrowing => call , apply, bind => create your own call, apply, bind prototype 3) Create your own map in js 4) strict mode

5) inspect tab

-> React js 1) control component and uncontrol components 2) create your own custom hook

3) diff usememo , usecallback and memo
item.author: tarunsankhla

item.title: SQL-Quick Tip #10 - 选择表定义 | SQL-Quick tip #10 - Select table definition
item.description:
艾伦·西蒙森

发表于这是我一年来积累的一系列快速提示和技巧的一部分，我认为它们对其他人有用。
如果您有类似的简短提示和技巧，请发表评论。

选择表定义

您是否曾经有过记录整个数据库或者只是一些程序员几年前创建的几个表的任务。 我有，而且我很快发现通过查询直接选择表的模式可以节省大量时间。 或者您可能无法访问生产数据库，但您遇到的错误或性能问题表明缺少索引或列定义错误，因此您需要向 IT 运营部门发送请求以获取有关数据库中的表为您服务。 在这两种情况下，下面的查询都非常有用。 

 DECLARE @ tableName VARCHAR ( 100 ) = 'Customers' SELECT table_name , ordinal_position , column_name , data_type , character_maximum_length , is_nullable FROM information_schema . columns WHERE table_name = @ tableName SELECT table_name , constraint_name FROM information_schema . constraint_table_usage WHERE table_name = @ tableName SELECT name , type_desc , is_unique , is_primary_key FROM sys . indexes WHERE object_id = OBJECT_ID ( @ tableName )

伊曼纽尔·巴托莱西 - Feb 24 2月24日

Sandor Drieënhuizen - Sandor Drieënhuizen - Feb 21 2月21日

Apache ShardingSphere - Apache ShardingSphere - Feb 21 2月21日



原文:

Allan Simonsen

Posted on Apr 26

This is part of a series of quick tips and tricks I have accumulated over the year, that I think can be useful for others.
If you have similar short tips and tricks please leave a comment.

Select table definition

Have you ever had the task of documenting a whole database or maybe just a few tables some programmer created years ago. I have, and I quickly found that having a query to directly select the schema of a table was a huge timesaver. Or maybe you don't have access to the production database, but the bugs or performance issues you experience, indicate a missing index or a wrong column definition, so you need to send a request for the IT operations department to fetch the information about a table from the database for you. In both cases the queries below can be very useful.

DECLARE @tableName VARCHAR(100) = 'Customers' SELECT table_name, ordinal_position, column_name, data_type, character_maximum_length, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = @tableName SELECT table_name, constraint_name FROM information_schema.constraint_table_usage WHERE table_name = @tableName SELECT name, type_desc, is_unique, is_primary_key FROM sys.indexes WHERE object_id = OBJECT_ID(@tableName)

Emanuele Bartolesi - Feb 24

Sandor Drieënhuizen - Feb 21

Apache ShardingSphere - Feb 21
item.author: coderallan

item.title: 使用 Lightrun 在生产环境中调试 Spring 事务 | Spring Transaction Debugging in Production with Lightrun
item.description:
由于其声明式事务管理，Spring 使构建可靠的应用程序变得更加容易。 它还支持程序化事务管理，但这并不常见。 在本文中，我想专注于声明式事务管理的角度，因为与编程方法相比，它似乎更难调试。

这是部分正确的。 我们不能在事务注释上放置断点。 但我正在超越自己。

什么是 Spring 的方法声明式事务管理？

在编写spring方法或类时，我们可以使用注解来声明一个方法或一个bean（类）是事务性的。 这个注解让我们可以使用属性来调整事务语义。 这让我们可以定义行为，例如：

  • 事务隔离级别——让我们解决诸如脏读、不可重复读、幻读等问题。
  • 事务管理器
  • 传播行为——我们可以定义事务是强制的、必需的等。这显示了方法是否期望接收事务以及它的行为方式
  • 但是当它被支持时，它是一个出色的性能/可靠性调整功能

以及更多。

事务不是和数据库驱动有关吗？

事务方法的概念对于新的 Spring 开发人员来说非常混乱。 事务是数据库驱动程序/JDBC 连接的一个特性，而不是一个方法。 为什么要在方法中声明呢？

还有更多。 其他功能，例如消息队列，也是事务性的。 我们可能会使用多个数据库。 在这些情况下，如果一个事务被回滚，我们需要回滚所有底层事务。 因此，我们在用户代码中进行事务管理，Spring 将其无缝传播到各种底层事务资源中。

如果我们不使用数据库 API，我们如何编写程序化事务管理？

该管理器包括编排各种资源的所有逻辑。

您可以将该管理器注入到典型的 spring 类中，但是像这样的 Java 代码编写声明性事务管理要容易得多：

 @Transactional public void myMethod () { // ... }

我在方法级别使用了注释，但我可以将它放在类级别。 类定义默认值，方法可以覆盖它。

这允许极大的灵活性，并且非常适合将业务代码与低级 JDBC 事务细节分开。

动态代理、面向方面的编程和注释

调试事务的关键是spring实现这个逻辑的方式。 实际上，这意味着当您在MyObject或MyClass上调用myMethod时，spring 会在它们之间创建一个代理类和一个代理对象实例。

因此，事务代理负责验证事务状态并执行它。

使用 Lightrun 调试 Spring 事务管理

重要提示：我假设您熟悉 Lightrun 基础知识。 如果没有，请阅读此内容。

程序化事务管理是微不足道的。 我们可以将快照放置在它开始或回滚的位置以获取状态。

但是如果注解失败，该方法将不会被调用，我们也不会得到回调。

不过，注释并不神奇。 正如我们上面讨论的，Spring 使用代理对象。 该代理机制调用通用代码，我们可以使用它来绑定快照。 一旦我们在那里绑定快照，我们就可以检测堆栈中的代理类型。 不幸的是，调试代理机制是有问题的，因为没有要调试的物理代码。 代理机制中的所有内容都是在运行时动态生成的。 幸运的是，这没什么大不了的。 没有这个，我们有足够的钩子进行调试。

查找实际事务类

我们需要做的第一件事是寻找实现事务功能的类。 打开 IntelliJ/IDEA 类视图（Command-O 或 CTRL-O）可以让我们按名称定位类。 输入“交易”导致以下视图：

这可能看起来很多，但我们需要一个具体的公共类。 所以注解和接口可以忽略。 由于我们只关心 Spring 类，我们可以忽略其他包。 尽管如此，我们正在寻找的课程在列表中相对较低，所以我花了一些时间才找到它。

在这种情况下，有趣的类是TransactionAspectSupport 。 打开课程后，我们需要选择下载课程源代码的选项。

完成后，我们可以寻找适用的公共方法。 在那里放置快照给了我一个提示：

我这里没有太多信息，但是堆栈中的invokeWithinTransaction方法是完美的！

继续使用该方法，我想在findById方法上跟踪特定于事务的信息：

为了将范围限制为findById ，我们添加了条件：

 method . getName (). equals ( "findById" )

一旦方法被命中，我们可以在堆栈中看到事务的详细信息。

如果您进一步滚动该方法，您可以看到在线程等发生异常时设置快照的理想位置。这是调试事务失败的重要中心点。

快照的好处之一是它们可以轻松调试并发事务。 它们的非阻塞特性使它们成为理想的工具。

这显着简化了应用程序的开发，并将对象逻辑与低级事务行为细节分开。

因为它们是生成的，我们不能真正直接调试它们，但我们可以调试类，它们在内部使用。 具体来说： TransactionAspectSupport就是一个很好的例子。

这意味着与并发相关的问题可以在 Lightrun 中重现。 此处讨论的所有内容都可以使用Lightrun 的免费版本来完成。



原文:

Spring makes building a reliable application much easier thanks to its declarative transaction management. It also supports programmatic transaction management, but that’s not as common. In this article, I want to focus on the declarative transaction management angle, since it seems much harder to debug compared to the programmatic approach.

This is partially true. We can’t put a breakpoint on a transactional annotation. But I’m getting ahead of myself.

What is Spring’s Method Declarative Transaction Management?

When writing a spring method or class, we can use annotations to declare that a method or a bean (class) is transactional. This annotation lets us tune transactional semantics using attributes. This lets us define behavior such as:

  • Transaction isolation levels – lets us address issues such as dirty reads, non-repeatable reads, phantom reads, etc.
  • Transaction Manager
  • Propagation behavior – we can define whether the transaction is mandatory, required, etc. This shows whether the method expects to receive a transaction and how it behaves
  • readOnly attribute – the DB does not always support a read-only transaction. But when it is supported, it’s an excellent performance/reliability tuning feature

And much more.

Isn’t the Transaction Related to the Database Driver?

The concept of transactional methods is very confusing to new spring developers. Transactions are a feature of the database driver/JDBC Connection, not of a method. Why declare it in the method?

There’s more to it. Other features, such as message queues, are also transactional. We might work with multiple databases. In those cases, if one transaction is rolled back, we need to rollback all the underlying transactions. As a result, we do the transaction management in user code and spring seamlessly propagates it into the various underlying transactional resource.

How can we Write Programmatic Transaction Management if we don’t use the Database API?

Spring includes a transaction manager that exposes the API’s we typically expect to see: begin, commit and rollback. This manager includes all the logic to orchestrate the various resources.

You can inject that manager to a typical spring class, but it’s much easier to just write declarative transaction management like this Java code:

@Transactional
public void myMethod() { // ...
}

I used the annotation on the method level, but I could have placed it on the class level. The class defines the default and the method can override it.

This allows for extreme flexibility and is great for separating business code from low level JDBC transaction details.

Dynamic Proxy, Aspect Oriented Programming and Annotations

The key to debugging transactions is the way spring implements this logic. Spring uses a proxy mechanism to implement the aspect oriented programming declarative capabilities. Effectively, this means that when you invoke myMethod on MyObject or MyClass spring creates a proxy class and a proxy object instance between them.

Spring routes your invocation through the proxy types which implement all the declarative annotations. As such, a transactional proxy takes care of validating the transaction status and enforcing it.

Debugging a Spring Transaction Management using Lightrun

IMPORTANT: I assume you’re familiar with Lightrun basics. If not, please read this.

Programmatic transaction management is trivial. We can just place a snapshot where it begins or is rolled back to get the status.

But if an annotation fails, the method won’t be invoked and we won’t get a callback.

Annotations aren’t magic, though. Spring uses a proxy object, as we discussed above. That proxy mechanism invokes generic code, which we can use to bind a snapshot. Once we bind a snapshot there, we can detect the proxy types in the stack. Unfortunately, debugging proxying mechanisms is problematic since there’s no physical code to debug. Everything in proxying mechanisms is generated dynamically at runtime. Fortunately, this isn’t a big deal. We have enough hooks for debugging without this.

Finding the Actual Transaction Class

The first thing we need to do is look for the class that implements transaction functionality. Opening the IntelliJ/IDEA class view (Command-O or CTRL-O) lets us locate a class by name. Typing in “Transaction” resulted in the following view:

This might seem like a lot, but we need a concrete public class. So annotations and interfaces can be ignored. Since we only care about Spring classes, we can ignore other packages. Still, the class we are looking for was relatively low in the list, so it took me some time to find it.

In this case, the interesting class is TransactionAspectSupport. Once we open the class, we need to select the option to download the class source code.

Once this is done, we can look for an applicable public method. getTransactionManager seemed perfect, but it’s a bit too bare. Placing a snapshot there provided me a hint:

I don’t have much information here but the invokeWithinTransaction method up the stack is perfect!

Moving on to that method, I would like to track information specific to a transaction on the findById method:

To limit the scope only to findById we add the condition:

method.getName().equals("findById")

Once the method is hit, we can see the details of the transaction in the stack.

If you scroll further in the method, you can see ideal locations to set snapshots in case of an exception in thread, etc. This is a great central point to debug transaction failures.

One of the nice things with snapshots is that they can easily debug concurrent transactions. Their non-blocking nature makes them the ideal tool for that.

TL;DR

Declarative configuration in Spring makes transactional operations much easier. This significantly simplifies the development of applications and separates the object logic from low level transactional behavior details.

Spring uses class-based proxies to implement annotations. Because they are generated, we can’t really debug them directly, but we can debug the classes, they use internally. Specifically: TransactionAspectSupport is a great example.

An immense advantage of Lightrun is that it doesn’t suspend the current thread. This means issues related to concurrency can be reproduced in Lightrun. Everything discussed here can be accomplished with the free version of Lightrun.
item.author: codenameone

item.title: 无需离开 IDE 即可部署您的第一个网站 | Deploy Your First Website without Leaving Your IDE
item.description:
发表于我一直想构建一个 VS 代码扩展，但我从来没有理由或足够的时间。 现在我在 GitHub 的 DevRel 工作，我终于有机会这样做了！ 我与 VS Code 团队合作创建了一个 VS Code 扩展，使您能够在不离开 IDE 的情况下将静态网页（Jekyll 或 HTML）快速部署到 GitHub Pages。

我最喜欢的两件事是产生积极的影响和尝试我没有使用过的技术！

这个怎么运作

用户体验和工作流程仍有改进的空间。 尽管如此，对于我使用我构建的扩展将代码部署到 GitHub Pages 的初始迭代，您可以采取以下步骤：

  • 您也可以添加 CSS 和 JavaScript。 对于这篇博文，我将创建一个 HTML 文件。 我创建了一个名为 index.html 的文件，其中包含一个 h1 元素，其中包括电影 Spiderman: Intro to Spiderverse 中的一句话和电影中的图像。

  • 不要忘记提交您的文件。 下面的截图表明我点击了“提交新文件”按钮来提交我新创建的 index.html 文件。

  • 安装名为“Deploy to GitHub Pages”的 VS Code 扩展。 为了找到扩展，我单击了 Visual Studio Code 中的扩展图标并搜索“Deploy to GitHub Pages”。 我为那个特定的扩展按下了安装。

  • 打开搜索栏以搜索新文件。 您可以通过以下方法做到这一点：
    • 使用转到菜单下的转到文件
    • 在 Windows 上使用此键盘快捷键 Ctrl+p 或 Ctrl+e
    • 在 macOS Cmd 上使用此键盘快捷键 ⌘+p
    • 在 Linux 上使用此键盘快捷键 Ctrl+p 或 Ctrl+e

  • 通过在搜索栏中键入“>”来触发扩展。 如果它正常工作，您应该会看到“部署到 GitHub Pages”字样，如下图所示。

  • 选择“部署到 GitHub 页面”后，您将收到登录 GitHub 的提示。

  • 完成身份验证过程后，您的 IDE 将显示一个下拉列表，其中包含您最近创建的十个存储库。 在屏幕截图中，我的“ghpages-into-the-spiderverse”存储库位于列表顶部。

  • 选择存储库“ghpages-into-the-spiderverse”后，会出现一条 toast 消息，提示我将存储库发布到 GitHub Pages。 单击“发布”按钮以确认您要将存储库部署到 GitHub Pages。

  • 检查操作日志以了解进度。 如果您看到所有绿色复选标记，如下图所示，您的网站已上线！

开放供稿

老实说，我没有投入尽可能多的时间来创建这个扩展。 有改进的余地。 我愿意为帮助我改进自述文件、改进工作流程等做出贡献。 随时在此存储库中打开问题和拉取请求： https ://github.com/blackgirlbytes/vscodeextension-deploy-to-gh-pages

Shivang Bhandari - Shivang Bhandari - Apr 21 4月21日



原文:

Rizèl Scarlett for GitHub

Posted on Apr 26

I’ve always wanted to build a VS code extension, but I never had a reason or enough time. Now that I work in DevRel at GitHub, I finally got the opportunity to do so! The Microsoft VS Code team runs a resume website workshop where participants deploy a resume built with HTML and CSS to GitHub Pages.

I collaborated with the VS Code team to create a VS Code Extension that enables you to quickly deploy your static web page (Jekyll or HTML) to GitHub Pages without leaving your IDE.

My two favorite things are making a positive impact and experimenting with technologies I haven’t used!

How it works

There’s still room for improvement with the user experience and the workflow. Still, for my initial iteration to deploy your code to GitHub Pages using the extension I built, you can take the following steps:

  • **Create an index.html file **at the very least. You can add CSS and JavaScript, too. For this blog post, I will create an HTML file. I created a file called index.html with an h1 element that includes a quote from the movie Spiderman: Intro to Spiderverse and an image from the movie as well.

  • Don’t forget to commit your file. The screenshot below indicates that I clicked the “Commit new file” button to commit my newly created index.html file.

  • Install the VS Code extension called “Deploy to GitHub Pages.” To find the extension, I clicked the extensions icon in Visual Studio Code and searched for “Deploy to GitHub Pages.” I pressed install for that particular extension.

  • Open the search bar to search for new files. You can do this through the following methods:
    • Using Go to File, which is under the Go menu
    • Using this keyboard shortcut on Windows Ctrl+p or Ctrl+e
    • Using this keyboard shortcut on macOS Cmd ⌘+p
    • Using this keyboard shortcut on Linux Ctrl+p or Ctrl+e

  • Trigger the extension by typing “>” in the search bar. If it works correctly, you should see the words “Deploy to GitHub Pages” as shown in the image below.

  • Once you choose the “Deploy to GitHub Pages”, you will receive a prompt to sign into GitHub.

  • After you complete the authentication process, your IDE will reveal a dropdown with your ten most recently created repositories. In the screenshot, my “ghpages-into-the-spiderverse” repository is at the top of the list.

  • After choosing the repository, “ghpages-into-the-spiderverse”, a toast message appears, prompting me to publish the repository to GitHub Pages. Click the “Publish” button to confirm that you want to deploy the repository to GitHub Pages.

  • The toast will update with a message that your site will go live in a few minutes.

  • Check the Actions logs for progress. GitHub Pages uses an action to build and deploy the site. If you see all green checkmarks, like the image below, your site is live!

Open to Contributions

Honestly, I didn’t invest as much time as I wanted into creating this extension. There’s room for improvement. I’m open to contributions in helping me improve my README, improving the workflow, and more. Feel free to open up an issue and a pull request in this repository: https://github.com/blackgirlbytes/vscodeextension-deploy-to-gh-pages

Shivang Bhandari - Apr 21
item.author: blackgirlbytes

item.title: [Conceito] - CQRS | [Conceito] - CQRS
item.description:
开发者，

Quer conhecer a história da @RaiiCorreia e como o CQRS melhorou sua vida? Quer conhecer a história da @RaiiCorreia e como o CQRS melhorou sua vida？ Acompanha esse fio que vc vai terminar essa thread abafando sobre o que é CQRS e vai fazer muito sucesso nas festas de crianças e almoços em família! Acompanha esse fio que vc vai terminar essa thread abafando sobre o que é CQRS e vai fazer muito sucesso nas festas de crianças e almoços em família！

Raissa é uma mulher muito bonita e por isso recebia muitas DMs pra possíveis contatinhos. Raissa é uma mulher muito bonita e por isso recebia muitas DMs pra possíveis contatinhos。 Como era muito educada, sempre conversava e guardava os contatos dos pretendentes – nunca se sabe o dia de amanhã, né? Como era muito educada, semper conversava e guardava os contatos dos fakeentes – nunca se sabe o dia de amanhã, né?

Raissa tinha muitas amigas e muitas delas eram super encalhadas. Raissa tinha muitas amigas e muitas delas eram super encalhadas。 Como Raissa era uma boa pessoa, sempre repassava seus contatinhos para suas amigas. Como Raissa 时代 uma boa pessoa，semper repassava seus contatinhos para suas amigas。 O problema é que não bastavam ser encalhadas, algumas amigas eram bem exigentes e queriam contatinhos bem específicos. O problema é que não bastavam ser encalhadas，algumas amigas eram bem exigentes e queriam contatinhos bem específicos。 🙄 🙄

O problema é que aquilo consumia muito Raissa. O problema é que aquilo consumia muito Raissa。 Ela ficava cansada de misturar as requisições que alteravam o estado de seu banco de dados – os comandos –, e as requisições de consultas – as queries. Ela ficava cansada de misturar as requisições que alteravam o estado de seu banco de dados – os comandos –, e as requisições de Consultas – 作为查询。

Raissa então lembrou-se de ter ouvido numa festa super descolada alguém falar sobre CQRS e que aquilo significava Command/Query Responsibility Segregation. Raissa então lembrou-se de ter ouvido numa festa super descolada alguém falar sobre CQRS e que aquilo significava 命令/查询职责分离。 Ou seja, Separação da Responsabilidade de Comandos e Consultas. Ou seja，Separação da Responsabilidade de Comandos e Consultas。

Os olhos de Raissa se encheram de esperança. Os olhos de Raissa se encheram de esperança。 🥲 🥲

Logo começou a modelar sua aplicação e bancos para que as consultas e comandos ficassem em lugares diferentes, inclusive em bancos diferentes para que pudessem escalar de forma independente. Logo começou a modelar sua aplicação e bancos para que as Consultas e comandos ficassem em lugares diferentes, inclusive em bancos diferentes para que pudessem escalar de forma Independente。

Ainda faltava uma peça para que tudo se encaixasse em seu novo desenho. Ainda faltava uma peça para que tudo se encaixasse em seu novo desenho。 Raissa então desenvolveu um componente capaz de realizar a sincronização entre os modelos de comando e consulta – algo simples capaz de atender sua demanda imediata. Raissa então desenvolveu um componente capaz de realizar a sincronização entre os modelos de comando e consulta – algo simples capaz de atender sua demanda imediata。

Após ter colocado sua nova aplicação em produção, Raissa se sentiu livre e satisfeita pois poderia atender as demandas dos novos contatinhos e de suas amigas exigentes e encalhadas. Após ter colocado sua nova aplicação em produção, Raissa se sentiu livre e satisfeita pois poderia atender as demandas dos novos contatinhos e de suas amigas exigentes e encalhadas。 E o melhor de tudo, de forma independente e escalável! E o melhor de tudo, de forma Independente e escalável！

Claro, esse é um exemplo super simples sobre o que é CQRS, mas a ideia fundamental realmente é separar os modelos de consultas e de comandos. Claro, esse é um exemplo super simples sobre o que é CQRS, mas a ideia basic realmente é separar os modelos de Consultas e de comandos。 É uma ideia simples, no final das contas. É uma ideia simples, no final das contas。

O banco não precisa ser necessariamente separado – separar em tabelas poderia ser um primeiro passo, por exemplo. O banco não precisa ser necessariamente separado – separar em tabelas poderia ser um primeiro passo, por exemplo。 Assim como a aplicação que poderia ser separada em duas unidades de deploy em vez de uma. Assim como a aplicação que poderia ser separada em duas unidades de deploy em vez de uma。

CQRS não dita sobre separações lógicas e físicas – isso cabe a você. CQRS não dita sobre separações lógicas e físicas – isso cabe a você。

Lembre-se de que quanto mais desacoplados, possivelmente mais complexos e também escaláveis seus sistemas podem ser. Lembre-se de que quto mais desacoplados, possivelmente mais complexos e também escaláveis seus sistemas podem ser。

É tudo uma questão de trade-off e bom senso. É tudo uma questão de trade-off e bom senso。

Algo que não mencionei nessa thread é sobre Event Sourcing. Algo que não mencionei nessa thread é sobre Event Sourcing。 Event Sourcing é comumente associado a CQRS, mas essas duas técnicas podem ser aplicadas separadamente. Event Sourcing é comumente associado a CQRS, mas essas duas técnicas podem ser aplicadas separadamente。 Não acredite que uma dependa da outra, ok? Não acredite que uma dependa da outra，好吗？

https://martinfowler.com/eaaDev/EventSourcing.html https://martinfowler.com/eaaDev/EventSourcing.html

Segue um link que explica sobre CQRS de outra forma. Segue um link que explica sobre CQRS de outra forma。

https://martinfowler.com/bliki/CQRS.html https://martinfowler.com/bliki/CQRS.html

Não preciso nem dizer, né? Não preciso nem dizer, né? Dá um abraço por ter lido até aqui, vai 🤗 Dá um abraço port ter lido até aqui, vai 🤗

E @RaiiCorreia, obrigado demais por ter topado esse mico de ser a protagonista dessa thread! E @RaiiCorreia，obrigado demais port ter topado esse mico de ser 一个主角 dessa 线程！ ❤️ ❤️



原文:

Conteúdo original nessa thread do Twitter

Ei dev,

Quer conhecer a história da @RaiiCorreia e como o CQRS melhorou sua vida? Acompanha esse fio que vc vai terminar essa thread abafando sobre o que é CQRS e vai fazer muito sucesso nas festas de crianças e almoços em família!

Raissa é uma mulher muito bonita e por isso recebia muitas DMs pra possíveis contatinhos. Como era muito educada, sempre conversava e guardava os contatos dos pretendentes – nunca se sabe o dia de amanhã, né?

Raissa tinha muitas amigas e muitas delas eram super encalhadas. Como Raissa era uma boa pessoa, sempre repassava seus contatinhos para suas amigas. O problema é que não bastavam ser encalhadas, algumas amigas eram bem exigentes e queriam contatinhos bem específicos. 🙄

O problema é que aquilo consumia muito Raissa. Ela ficava cansada de misturar as requisições que alteravam o estado de seu banco de dados – os comandos –, e as requisições de consultas – as queries.

Raissa então lembrou-se de ter ouvido numa festa super descolada alguém falar sobre CQRS e que aquilo significava Command/Query Responsibility Segregation. Ou seja, Separação da Responsabilidade de Comandos e Consultas.

Os olhos de Raissa se encheram de esperança. 🥲

Logo começou a modelar sua aplicação e bancos para que as consultas e comandos ficassem em lugares diferentes, inclusive em bancos diferentes para que pudessem escalar de forma independente.

Ainda faltava uma peça para que tudo se encaixasse em seu novo desenho. Raissa então desenvolveu um componente capaz de realizar a sincronização entre os modelos de comando e consulta – algo simples capaz de atender sua demanda imediata.

Após ter colocado sua nova aplicação em produção, Raissa se sentiu livre e satisfeita pois poderia atender as demandas dos novos contatinhos e de suas amigas exigentes e encalhadas. E o melhor de tudo, de forma independente e escalável!

Claro, esse é um exemplo super simples sobre o que é CQRS, mas a ideia fundamental realmente é separar os modelos de consultas e de comandos. É uma ideia simples, no final das contas.

O banco não precisa ser necessariamente separado – separar em tabelas poderia ser um primeiro passo, por exemplo. Assim como a aplicação que poderia ser separada em duas unidades de deploy em vez de uma.

CQRS não dita sobre separações lógicas e físicas – isso cabe a você.

Lembre-se de que quanto mais desacoplados, possivelmente mais complexos e também escaláveis seus sistemas podem ser.

É tudo uma questão de trade-off e bom senso.

Algo que não mencionei nessa thread é sobre Event Sourcing. Event Sourcing é comumente associado a CQRS, mas essas duas técnicas podem ser aplicadas separadamente. Não acredite que uma dependa da outra, ok?

https://martinfowler.com/eaaDev/EventSourcing.html

Segue um link que explica sobre CQRS de outra forma.

https://martinfowler.com/bliki/CQRS.html

Não preciso nem dizer, né? Dá um abraço por ter lido até aqui, vai 🤗

E @RaiiCorreia, obrigado demais por ter topado esse mico de ser a protagonista dessa thread! ❤️
item.author: zanfranceschi

item.title: #100DaysofCode Day[1](2)（不是另一个 #100DaysofCode 博客，但实际上是） | #100DaysofCode Day[1](2) (Not another #100DaysofCode blog, but it actually is)
item.description:
你好！

这是我第一次进入我的博客以参加 #100DaysofCode 挑战。 对于那些不知道的人来说，#100DaysofCode 挑战本质上是尝试将一天中至少一个小时用于编码的试验。 这项挑战的一个规则是，在工作时间内完成的编码不会计入您的进度。

您可能想知道为什么标题中有三个数字。 嗯，第一个是 100，我们将编码的天数（duh）。 计算机从 0 开始计数，从技术上讲，这是我参加 #100DaysofCode 挑战的第二天。 我从 2022 年 4 月 24 日星期日开始，但并没有真正考虑记录我登上 Mt. #100DaysofCode 的顶峰，所以既然是第 2 天，我们就有 [1]。 最后，最简单的最后，是实际的一天。 现在是第 2 天。

这将主要是一个 Python 博客，正如我在精彩的 App Brewery 的 100 天代码：2022 年完整的 Python Pro 训练营中关注的那样。（旁注：我知道我没有包含主题标签。我想知道是不是是课程所在网站的限制还是谁知道？）

在第一天，我们最终构建了一个乐队名称生成器，它可以输入您出生在哪个城市和您宠物的名字。

乐队名称生成器

今天，Day[1]，更多地涉及学习数据类型。 我有基本的编程知识，并且熟悉基本类型，所以在那里学习并不多。

浮点数、布尔值、int、str

但是，我确实学到的东西非常整洁。 查看下面的提示计算器：

小费计算器

我最挣扎的事情是试图弄清楚如何计算小费的百分比。 我知道你会在你想给小费的百分比前面加上一个小数，但是在我面前把它变成代码有点困难（数学很难）。 我终于环顾四周，意识到我可以将这个数字除以 100。

我想在未来重新审视这个。 最大的原因是，我想知道如何将用户限制在程序中提供的小费金额（12、15、20 [PS 请慷慨地给您的服务器小费！]）。 实际上，输入它让我想重新考虑并至少将最小值保持在 15%。

据我了解，您可以在某些情况下混合和匹配数据类型，这就是其中之一。

这肯定花了我一个多小时，只是因为一些交互式编码挑战和我在 Wordpress、Medium 和 Blogger 之间来回跳来决定我想把这些东西放在哪里。 我现在已经选择了 Medium，并将继续进行相应的评估。

感谢那些陪伴我这么久的人，谢谢。 我不希望所有帖子都超长。 由于这是对昨天帖子的介绍/回顾，因此我认为正常的一天会包含更多内容。 这是明天，在#100DaysofCode 挑战的阶梯上向上移动。



原文:

Hello!

This is my first entry into my blog for the #100DaysofCode challenge. For those that are unaware, the #100DaysofCode challenge is essentially a trial of trying to commit at least an hour of your day to coding. One rule of this challenge is that coding done during work hours, will not count towards your progress.

You might be wondering why there are three numbers in the title. Well, the first is 100, the amount of days we’ll be coding (duh). Computers start counting at 0, and technically this is my second day of the #100DaysofCode challenge. I started on Sunday, April 24 2022 but didn’t really think about documenting my climb up the summit of Mt. #100DaysofCode, so since it’s Day 2, we have [1]. Finally, the easiest for last, is the actual day. It’s day 2.

This will mostly be a Python blog, as I follow along in the wonderful App Brewery’s 100 Days of Code: The Complete Python Pro Bootcamp for 2022. (Side note: I know I didn’t include the hashtag. I’m wondering if it was a limitation of the site the course is on or who knows what?)

Day[0] was really just an intro to the course and going over some Python basics. For the first day, we ended up building a band name generator that takes your input of what city you were born in and your pet’s name.

band name generator

Today, Day[1], was bit more involved learning about data types. I have a basic programming knowledge, and am familiar with the basic types so it wasn’t much to pick up there.

float, Boolean, int, str

However, the things that I did learn were pretty neat. Check out this tip calculator below:

tip calculator

The thing I struggled with the most was trying to figure out how to calculate the percentage of a tip. I know that you throw a decimal in front of the percentage you want to tip, but getting that into code in front of me was a bit tough (math is hard guys). I had finally looked around a bit and realized I could divide that number by 100.

I would like to revisit this in the future. The biggest reason for that being, I’d like to know how to limit users to the provided tip amounts in the program (12, 15, 20 [P.S. PLEASE TIP YOUR SERVERS GENEROUSLY!]). Actually, typing that out makes me want to reconsider and at least keep the minimum to 15%.

fStrings are also a cool concept that was in this lesson, allowing us to throw in a variable into a string if that variable happens to be a different data type. From what I understand, you can mix and match data types in certain situations and this was one of them.

This definitely took me longer than an hour, only because of a few of the interactive coding challenges and me bouncing back and forth between Wordpress, Medium, and Blogger deciding where I want to put this stuff. I’ve settled on Medium for now and will continue to evaluate accordingly.

To those of you that stuck with me this long, thanks. I don’t expect all posts to be super long. Since this was kind of an introductory/recap of yesterday post, there’s a bit more than I would think a normal day would consist of. Here’s to tomorrow and moving up another rung on the ladder that is the #100DaysofCode challenge.
item.author: keatonpalmer

item.title: 将预先计算的路由传递给 Ember 中的链接助手 | Passing pre-computed routes to link-to helper in Ember
item.description:
西瓦苏布拉曼尼亚

发表于句法

{{link-to routeName dynamicSegment1 dynamicSegment2 (query-params key1=value1 key2=value2)}} (or) {{link-to params=linkParameters}}

描述

考虑具有以下路线的照片应用程序

this . route ( ' photos ' , function () { this . route ( ' resolution ' , { path : ' /:value ' }); });

计算组件中的链接

linkParametersVariation1 : computed ({ get () { let resolution = get ( this , ' resolution); return { ' routeName ' : ' photos / resolution ' , dynamicSegment: resolution, page: 1, perPage: 30 } } }), linkParametersVariation2: computed({ get() { let resolution = get(this, ' resolution ); return [ ' photos/resolution ' , resolution , { isQueryParams : true , values : { page : 1 , perPage : 30 } }] } })

在模板中使用链接

{{#link-to linkParametersVariation1.routName linkParametersVariation1.dynamicSegment (query-params page=linkParametersVariation1.page perPage=linkParametersVariation1.perPage)}} <div> variation1 </div> {{/link-to}} {{#link-to params=linkParametersVariation2}} <div> variation2 </div> {{/link-to}}



原文:

Sivasubramanian

Posted on Apr 26

Syntax

{{link-to routeName  dynamicSegment1 dynamicSegment2 (query-params key1=value1 key2=value2)}}

(or)

{{link-to params=linkParameters}}

Description

Consider a photo application with below routes

this.route('photos', function() { this.route('resolution', { path: '/:value' });
});

Compute links in component

linkParametersVariation1: computed({ get() { let resolution = get(this, 'resolution); return { 'routeName': 'photos/resolution', dynamicSegment: resolution, page: 1, perPage: 30 } }
}), linkParametersVariation2: computed({ get() { let resolution = get(this, 'resolution); return ['photos/resolution', resolution, { isQueryParams: true, values: { page: 1, perPage: 30 } }] }
})

Using links in template

{{#link-to linkParametersVariation1.routName linkParametersVariation1.dynamicSegment (query-params page=linkParametersVariation1.page perPage=linkParametersVariation1.perPage)}} <div>variation1</div>
{{/link-to}} {{#link-to params=linkParametersVariation2}} <div>variation2</div>
{{/link-to}}

item.title: 我如何通过云简历挑战在 AWS 中培养实践技能 | How I Develop Practical Skills in AWS with the Cloud Resume Challenge
item.description:
马科斯·费雷罗

发表于我的旅途

在云简历挑战之前，我以前没有任何 AWS 服务的经验，作为我在迈阿密戴德学院的一门课程的一部分，通过学习 AWS 并获得 AWS 解决方案架构师认证，我遇到了在线云简历挑战 (Linked/Reddit)福雷斯特·布拉泽尔（Forrest Brazeal）。

挑战
我对 AWS 世界完全陌生，我于 2021 年 9 月获得了 AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner Certification。然后我最近获得了 AWS Certified Solutions Architect Associate（2022 年 4 月）。 我将继续增加我在 AWS 服务方面的基础，并计划很快为 AWS Certified Solution Architect Professional 做准备。

前端 - HTML / CSS

我找到了一个我喜欢的 Bootstrap 模板，并对其进行了修改，以通过删除额外的页面和链接来展示我的项目。 我尽量保持简单。 我以前没有 HTML/CSS 方面的经验，互联网帮助编辑了模板。 静态 S3 网站

根据我在准备 AWS 认证的大学课程中获得的知识，我学习了如何在 S3 上托管静态网站并使用 CloudFront 进行内容分发。 我使用 AWS Route 53 购买了一个域名，并将其配置为使用 CloudFront 分配。 此外，我使用 AWS Certification Manager (ACM) 为该站点获取 SSL 证书。

后端

动态数据库

我对 DynamoDB 有理论知识，但实践经验很少。 我创建了一个带有分区键（日期）和属性计数的表。 每天，该网页都有一定数量的访问者。
从表中检索项目并更新计数的代码，我在网上搜索并找到了 Python 的代码。

拉姆达

在研究并花时间研究文档之后，我用 Python 代码创建了一个函数来更新表中的计数，同时获取计数。 我为该函数添加了 IAM 角色以访问 DynamoDB 表并测试它是否正常工作。

这是另一个从未使用过的服务，但我了解了它，我熟悉 API 的概念，我了解它的目标和功能。 必须在 API Gateway 资源上启用 CORS（跨域资源共享）才能在调用响应时取回响应。

在此之后，后端完成。 我可以调用 API 链接，这会触发 Lambda 函数，它将计数增加 1 并返回值。

测试

我以前有过单元测试的经验，所以我为 Lambda 函数编写了一些测试。

此时，后端基础设施的创建使用 SAM 完全自动化，而前端则完全手动完成。

源代码控制

我从一开始就使用 git 并将我的代码和所有文件存储在 Github →( https://github.com/maf1978/CloudResumeChallenge.git )

我从未使用过 Github Actions，但我使用过类似的 CI/CD 工具，因此这一步非常快速且有趣。 我创建了我的模板并首先运行了 Lambda 函数的单元测试。 在第二步中，我使用预定义的 SAM 操作来构建基础设施并将其部署到 AWS。

在最后一步中，我将静态文件上传到 S3。

结论

这个项目真的很棒，它给了我一个尝试以前没有尝试过的 AWS 资源的目的，给了我在我缺乏技能的部分的经验，让我学到了一些非常有用的技能，让我感到很舒服AWS 中的项目。 我更有信心在 AWS 中承担更具挑战性的项目并获得更多实用技能。

感谢您的阅读！ 如果你想知道我的云冒险，

我的简历网站：( www.mafcloudsolutions.com )

我的 Github 回购与挑战：( https://github.com/maf1978/CloudResumeChallenge.git )

维罗妮卡·法蒂霍娃—— Apr 15 4月15日



原文:

Marcos Ferrero

Posted on Apr 26

My journey

I had not previous experience to any AWS services before the cloud resume challenge and as part of one of my class at Miami Dade College, through learning AWS and getting certified as AWS solution architect I came across Cloud Resume Challenge floated online (Linked/Reddit) by Forrest Brazeal.

The Challenge
AWS Certification

I was completely new to AWS world, I achieved AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner Certification in September 2021. Then I recently achieved AWS Certified Solutions Architect Associate (April 2022). I will continue increasing my foundation on AWs services and plan to prepare for AWS Certified Solution Architect Professional very soon.

Front end- HTML / CSS

I found a Bootstrap template I liked and modified it to showcase my projects by removing additional pages and links. I tried to keep it as simply as possible. I don't have previous experience in HTML/CSS before, and the internet helped with editing the template. The HTML page would also include a JavaScript snippet which will update and fetch the visitor count from the "back end".

Static S3 Website

Based on the knowledge I acquired during my college class preparation of AWS certifications, I learned how hosting a static website on S3 and using CloudFront for content distribution. I purchased a domain name using AWS Route 53 and configured it to use the CloudFront distribution. Also, I made use of AWS Certification Manager(ACM) to procure an SSL certificate for the site.

Back-end

DynamoDB

I had theoretical knowledge about DynamoDB but very little practical experience. I created a Table with a Partition key (date) and an attribute count. Each day, the webpage has a certain amount of visitors.
The code to retrieve items from a Table and to update the count, I searched it online and found the code for Python.

Lambda

The Lambda function was other challenge for me because had not experience. After researched and spend time researching for documentation, I created a function with Python code to update the count in the table, and at the same time get the count number. I added the IAM role for the function to access the DynamoDB table and tested that it was working.

API Gateway

This was another services that had never used, but I had learnt about it and I was familiar with the concept of an API, I understood its goal and function. The API Gateway exposes a REST API Endpoint, which will be called by Javascript snippet embedded in the front-end html page, on every page visit/refresh - to update and fetch the visitor count from a DynamoDB table, through Lambda function. Enabling CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) on the API Gateway resource is mandatory to fetch the response back, when it is called.

After this, the backend was done. I could call the API link, this triggered the Lambda function, which increased the count by 1 and returned the value.

Tests

I had previous experience with unit tests so I wrote some tests for the Lambda function.

**Infrastructure as Code

At this point, the backend infrastructure creation was fully automated with SAM and the frontend was done fully manually.

Source control

I used git from the beginning and stored my code and all my files in Github →(https://github.com/maf1978/CloudResumeChallenge.git)

CI/CD

I had never used Github Actions but I had used a similar CI/CD tool, so this step was quite fast and fun. I created my template and first ran the unit tests for the Lambda function. In the second step, I used the predefined SAM action to build and deploy the infrastructure to AWS.

In the last step, I upload the static files to S3.

Conclusion

This project was really amazing, it gave me a purpose for trying AWS resources I hadn’t tried before, gave me experience in the parts where I was lacking skills and made me learn a couple of very useful skills and made me feel comfortable with making projects in AWS. I feel much more confident to take on more challenging projects and get even more practical skills in AWS.

Thank you for reading! If you want to know my cloud adventures,

My CV website: (www.mafcloudsolutions.com)

My Github repo with the challenge:(https://github.com/maf1978/CloudResumeChallenge.git)

Veronika Fatikhova - Apr 15
item.author: maf1978
